jobs.title: GTM Recruiter, AMER
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Notion is the collaborative AI workspace where teams and agents think together. We're building one place where your knowledge, projects, meetings, and AI tools live side by side, so work is faster, clearer, and less fragmented. Millions of individuals, small teams, and large companies run their work on Notion.

Notinos (our employees) are customer zero in bringing this future of work to life. We care about craft, building things that last, and the belief that great work is still fundamentally human. Our goal isn’t to ship the next feature. Each and every team of Notinos is working to set the standard for how humans work together in the AI era. From building a business’s system of record to making and managing AI agents to automating away the busy work, we care deeply about giving our customers more time for their life’s work.

About The Role

The Notion Recruiting team acts as trusted business partners who craft a thorough, inclusive, and curated interviewing experience. We believe hiring exceptional talent, at the right time, is the best path forward in order to achieve our company’s ambitious goals.

We're looking for a GTM Recruiter to support our growth across key Go-to-Market (GTM) teams. In this role, you'll bring craft, clarity, and care to every search, balancing speed with sound judgment to help us grow intentionally.

This role is based in San Francisco, CA. We work from our offices on Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays (our Anchor Days) because we do our best thinking and building together in person. We’re looking for someone who’s excited to work alongside the team during those days.

What You'll Achieve

  • Partner with leaders and advisors across our GTM teams - including the Sales Organization - as a trusted thought partner and advisor.

  • Own the end-to-end recruiting process - from sourcing through close - creating a thoughtful, inclusive, and high-touch experience for each and every candidate.

  • Conduct market research and talent mapping to inform and evolve our hiring strategy.

  • Design structured, scalable hiring processes across intake, interview design, calibration, and debriefs - ensuring consistent and objective evaluation and assessment processes.

  • Utilize data and insights to influence hiring strategy - including pipeline health, time-to-fill, and offer acceptance rates.

  • Coach and consult hiring managers and interviewing teams to strengthen decision quality and mitigate bias.

  • Partner with cross-functional teams including Total Rewards, People Operations, People Partners, and Executive Operations.

  • Drive our diversity and inclusion strategy forward (internally called IDEA), with a heavy emphasis on sourcing candidates from under-represented backgrounds.

Skills You'll Need To Bring

  • 5+ year of full-cycle recruiting experience in a high-growth environment, with deep expertise on niche and/or complex GTM searches.

  • Sales Recruiters: 3+ years of full-cycle recruiting experience supporting Sales, preferably at a high-growth B2B SaaS technology startup

    • This includes but is not limited to: Revenue Operations, Solutions Engineering, Customer Success, Professional Services, and Sales.

  • Experience influencing senior leaders and coaching, advising, and consulting hiring managers, guiding them through our recruiting process to ensure they keep talent density top of mind

  • Operational rigor and comfort using data to guide decisions and measure impact.

  • Experience building diverse candidate pipelines and guiding hiring managers in creating inclusive teams

  • Outstanding communication, attention-to-detail, and interpersonal skills. You value your candidates' and hiring managers' time and are relentless with delivering a high-touch and quality recruiting experience.

  • Strong sourcing and closing instincts, backed by market fluency across GTM disciplines.

  • Collaborative, pro-active, and team-oriented - you clear obstacles and elevate those around you.

  • Energized by change and motivated by the chance to build in a scaling and growing environment.

  • You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you’re curious and willing to adopt AI tools to work smarter and deliver better results.

Notion is committed to providing highly competitive cash compensation, equity, and benefits. The compensation offered for this role will be based on multiple factors such as location, the role’s scope and complexity, and the candidate’s experience and expertise, and may vary from the range provided below. For roles based in San Francisco or New York City, the estimated base salary range for this role is $136,000 - $170,000 per year.

By clicking “Submit Application”, I understand and agree that Notion and its affiliates and subsidiaries will collect and process my information in accordance with Notion’s Global Recruiting Privacy Policy and NYLL 144. #LI-Onsite

You don’t need deep AI expertise for every role, but we do expect every Notino to be intellectually curious, drawn to tinkering and discovery, and excited to use AI as a real collaborator in their work. For some roles, AI fluency is a core requirement — when that’s the case, we'll say so explicitly in the qualifications. People who thrive here don’t treat AI as a novelty. They use it to think better, and make their work easier for others to build on.

Equal Opportunity & Accommodations

We hire talented people from a wide range of backgrounds. If you’re excited about this role but don’t meet every bullet, we still encourage you to apply. Notion is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of any legally protected characteristic. Consistent with applicable law, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Notion provides reasonable accommodations during the application process; if you need one, please let your recruiter know.

Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please let your recruiter know.

jobs.title: QA Manager
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Notion is the collaborative AI workspace where teams and agents think together. We're building one place where your knowledge, projects, meetings, and AI tools live side by side, so work is faster, clearer, and less fragmented. Millions of individuals, small teams, and large companies run their work on Notion.

Notinos (our employees) are customer zero in bringing this future of work to life. We care about craft, building things that last, and the belief that great work is still fundamentally human. Our goal isn’t to ship the next feature. Each and every team of Notinos is working to set the standard for how humans work together in the AI era. From building a business’s system of record to making and managing AI agents to automating away the busy work, we care deeply about giving our customers more time for their life’s work.

About the Role:

You’ll lead Notion’s central QA team and the QA roadmap, setting clear ownership and quality standards that scale across Notion. The focus isn’t a checklist of test cases—it’s improving real product quality outcomes, with strong judgment about where risk is building and how to catch it early. You’ll build and evolve CI/CD quality gates aligned to Notion’s reliability/quality goals, improve early detection, and partner across teams and platforms to keep our shipping velocity high without breaking trust.

This role is based in Hyderabad, India. We work from our offices on Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays (our Anchor Days) because we do our best thinking and building together in person. We’re looking for someone who’s excited to work alongside the team during those days.

What You'll Achieve:

  • Big bet: Make quality a shared, measurable system across Notion—clear standards, predictable signal, and fewer surprises as we ship.

  • By your 30th day: Establish a single QA roadmap + prioritization rubric used cross-team, and align partners on quality standards and decision rights.

  • By your 60th day: Define and roll out CI/CD release quality gates aligned to quality/reliability goals for critical pipelines; make CI signal more reliable (less flake, faster feedback) for top workflows.

  • By your 90th day: Use AI/agents to generate and maintain high-signal tests, automate detection → triage → recommended fixes, and package those workflows so teams can reuse them.

  • By end of year 1: Raise the bar with a stable, highly automated QA org that measurably reduces escaped critical issues/SLO breaks and expands into scalable performance + reliability test coverage for key workflows.

 

Skills You'll Need to Bring:

  • Experience leading QA teams and owning a QA roadmap focused on measurable quality outcomes.

  • Strong QA-first mindset with an software engineering orientation (roughly 70% QA / 30% engineering) — able to set standards and also build/ship the systems that enforce them.

  • Outcome ownership: focuses on measurable product quality improvements (risk reduction, fewer escapes, higher confidence) rather than “tools” or checklist-driven QA work; strong judgment for where risk is building and how to catch it early.

  • Strong grasp of CI/CD and how automated test suites run, fail, and gate releases in practice.

  • Comfortable with our QA automation stack and eager to learn/level it up; able to evaluate alternatives and propose pragmatic improvements.

  • Ability to design and implement QA automation frameworks; has written AI-assisted/AI-driven automation tests in code (not just tooling evaluation).

  • Understand how AI has shaped modern QA and can apply AI/agents to accelerate quality workflows (test generation/maintenance, flake reduction, triage, and prevention) while fitting into existing team momentum.

  • Fluency with observability fundamentals and tools (e.g., Datadog/Honeycomb): defining metrics, dashboards, and using signal to detect risk early.

  • Proven ability to operate in a central QA model—aligning multiple teams/platforms on shared quality standards, priorities, and clear ownership.

  • Coach and develop QA engineers: raises the bar through mentorship, feedback, and clear career ladders/expectations.

  • Strong cross-functional collaborator: builds tight partnerships across Engineering, Product, Design, Support, and other teams to stay ahead of changes and pain points across Notion.

 

By clicking “Submit Application”, I understand and agree that Notion and its affiliates and subsidiaries will collect and process my information in accordance with Notion’s Global Recruiting Privacy Policy.

 

#LI-Onsite

A Note on AI

You don’t need deep AI expertise for every role, but we do expect every Notino to be intellectually curious, drawn to tinkering and discovery, and excited to use AI as a real collaborator in their work. For some roles, AI fluency is a core requirement — when that’s the case, we'll say so explicitly in the qualifications. People who thrive here don’t treat AI as a novelty. They use it to think better, and make their work easier for others to build on.

Equal Opportunity & Accommodations

We hire talented people from a wide range of backgrounds. If you’re excited about this role but don’t meet every bullet, we still encourage you to apply. Notion is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of any legally protected characteristic. Consistent with applicable law, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Notion provides reasonable accommodations during the application process; if you need one, please let your recruiter know.

Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please let your recruiter know.

jobs.title: Account Executive, Commercial, MEDIT
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Notion is the collaborative AI workspace where teams and agents think together. We're building one place where your knowledge, projects, meetings, and AI tools live side by side, so work is faster, clearer, and less fragmented. Millions of individuals, small teams, and large companies run their work on Notion.

Notinos (our employees) are customer zero in bringing this future of work to life. We care about craft, building things that last, and the belief that great work is still fundamentally human. Our goal isn’t to ship the next feature. Each and every team of Notinos is working to set the standard for how humans work together in the AI era. From building a business’s system of record to making and managing AI agents to automating away the busy work, we care deeply about giving our customers more time for their life’s work.

About the Role

We’re hiring an Account Executive in Dublin to drive new customer acquisition and expansion across our Commercial (SMB) segment. This role is for a full-cycle seller who can run structured discovery calls, deliver compelling demos, navigate technical and business stakeholders, and progress complex opportunities to close—while operating with a strong outbound mindset and high urgency.

You’ll work with buyers such as CIO, CTO, VP Product and VP Engineering, and you’ll be expected to develop strong product fluency in technical use cases (APIs, data, developer tooling, infrastructure, and AI/agentic workflows).

What You'll Achieve

  • An outbound-first mentality, proactively build net new pipeline and move quickly to create momentum within accounts.

  • Own the full sales cycle, including prospecting, discovery, solution alignment, demoing, stakeholder management, negotiation, and close.

  • Run high-quality discovery calls to uncover business outcomes and technical requirements, translating them into a clear value narrative.

  • Deliver live demos and tailored storytelling to guide customers through evaluation, including light solution design as needed (workspace architecture, permissions, rollout approach, integrations) to unblock adoption.

  • Navigate complex commercial-stage requirements (security, legal, procurement) with support from cross-functional partners.

  • Forecast accurately and operate with consistent, high-velocity execution to meet or exceed quota.

  • Partner with Solutions, Product, and Marketing to bring customer insights back to the business.

Skills You'll Need to Bring

  • 1–3 years of closing experience in a full-cycle quota-carrying role (or adjacent role with clear closing exposure), with consistent attainment.

  • Experience selling into or working closely with technical buyer personas (e.g., CIO/CTO/VP Eng/VP Product), translating product capabilities into workflow and ROI narratives.

  • Outbound discipline, pipeline creativity and full-cycle sales capability, including discovery, demo and closing motions.

  • Demonstrated ability to run multi-threaded deals and influence across complex customer org structures

  • Curiosity and learning velocity—can pick up technical concepts quickly and explain them clearly.

  • Hands-on orientation with AI: uses AI to improve personal workflow and can speak to modern AI-enabled work patterns.

  • Fluency in Italian and English.

  • Comfortable with ambiguity and experience selling in the MEDIT market: you’ll help refine our commercial motion, build repeatable processes, and bring sharp customer feedback into how we scale.

Nice to Haves

  • Experience with usage-based / consumption pricing models.

  • Prior exposure to developer tooling, APIs, data, infrastructure, or AI products.

  • You've been an early sales hire at a fast growing start up before

  • Fluency in Spanish


Notion is committed to providing highly competitive cash compensation, equity, and benefits. The compensation offered for this role will be based on multiple factors such as location, the role's scope and complexity, and the candidate's experience and expertise, and may vary from the range provided below. The estimated range for total on target earnings (including base salary and on target incentive pay) for this role is €130,000 per year.

 - Own the full sales cycle, including prospecting, discovery, solution alignment, demoing, stakeholder management, negotiation, and close.

 - Run high-quality discovery calls to uncover business outcomes and technical requirements, translating them into a clear value narrative.

 - Deliver live demos and tailored storytelling to guide customers through evaluation, including light solution design as needed (workspace architecture, permissions, rollout approach, integrations) to unblock adoption.

 - Navigate complex commercial-stage requirements (security, legal, procurement) with support from cross-functional partners.

 - Forecast accurately and operate with consistent, high-velocity execution to meet or exceed quota.

 - Partner with Solutions, Product, and Marketing to bring customer insights back to the business.




SKILLS YOU'LL NEED TO BRING

 - 1–3 years of closing experience in a full-cycle quota-carrying role (or adjacent role with clear closing exposure), with consistent attainment.

 - Experience selling into or working closely with technical buyer personas (e.g., CIO/CTO/VP Eng/VP Product), translating product capabilities into workflow and ROI narratives.

 - Outbound discipline, pipeline creativity and full-cycle sales capability, including discovery, demo and closing motions.

 - Demonstrated ability to run multi-threaded deals and influence across complex customer org structures

 - Curiosity and learning velocity—can pick up technical concepts quickly and explain them clearly.

 - Hands-on orientation with AI: uses AI to improve personal workflow and can speak to modern AI-enabled work patterns.

 - Fluency in Italian and English.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and experience selling in the MEDIT market: you’ll help refine our commercial motion, build repeatable processes, and bring sharp customer feedback into how we scale.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with usage-based / consumption pricing models.

 - Prior exposure to developer tooling, APIs, data, infrastructure, or AI products.

 - You've been an early sales hire at a fast growing start up before

 - Fluency in Spanish


Notion is committed to providing highly competitive cash compensation, equity, and benefits. The compensation offered for this role will be based on multiple factors such as location, the role's scope and complexity, and the candidate's experience and expertise, and may vary from the range provided below. jobs.title: Solutions Engineer, Commercial
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Notion is the collaborative AI workspace where teams and agents think together. We're building one place where your knowledge, projects, meetings, and AI tools live side by side, so work is faster, clearer, and less fragmented. Millions of individuals, small teams, and large companies run their work on Notion.

Notinos (our employees) are customer zero in bringing this future of work to life. We care about craft, building things that last, and the belief that great work is still fundamentally human. Our goal isn’t to ship the next feature. Each and every team of Notinos is working to set the standard for how humans work together in the AI era. From building a business’s system of record to making and managing AI agents to automating away the busy work, we care deeply about giving our customers more time for their life’s work.

About the Role

Notion’s sales motion is evolving from product-led to sales-led. Many customers start self-serve, then partner with our team as they scale. As a Solutions Engineer focused on our Commercial segment, you’ll work primarily with companies of roughly 100–300 employees. You’ll pair closely with Account Executives to run discovery, deliver tailored demos, scope lightweight solutions, and guide prospects through evaluation, security, and deployment so they can adopt Notion quickly and successfully.

You’ll be joining a growing team and will have opportunities to improve our tools, content, and processes while staying close to customers.

What You’ll Do

  • Partner with AEs on customer engagements across the Commercial segment to understand goals and success criteria

  • Lead discovery, design solution approaches in Notion, and deliver persuasive product demos tailored to each prospect’s workflows

  • Advise on integrations and basic technical topics such as SSO/SAML, SCIM, and admin controls, escalating to specialists for deeper needs

  • Answer product and technical questions, and support standard security and procurement reviews with clear, timely responses

  • Create proof-of-concepts and example workspaces that showcase how Notion can replace point tools and support team-wide workflows

  • Capture and communicate structured product feedback to Product and Engineering

  • Contribute enablement content for the sales team, including demo assets, talk tracks, and competitive notes

  • Document repeatable patterns, field learnings, and best practices to strengthen our internal knowledge base

What You'll Achieve

  • Help Commercial customers evaluate, pilot, and launch Notion to meet their goals

  • Shorten sales cycles through crisp technical guidance and compelling demos

  • Improve our pre-sales playbooks and assets for the Commercial segment

  • Influence the roadmap by channeling customer insights

Skills You'll Need to Bring

  • 2+ years in a customer-facing technical role such as Solutions/Sales Engineer, Product Manager, Solution Consultant, or Implementation Specialist

  • Experience supporting mid-market or Commercial deals, ideally at a SaaS company

  • Ability to explain APIs and integrations at a conceptual level and map them to customer workflows

  • Familiarity with identity and security concepts such as SSO/SAML, SCIM, user provisioning, and basic security reviews

  • Strong written and verbal communication, with the ability to translate business needs into practical solution designs and clear next steps

  • Solid project coordination skills with the ability to run multiple evaluations in parallel

  • Adaptable, resourceful, and comfortable working in a fast-changing environment

  • You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you’re curious and willing to adopt AI tools to work smarter and deliver better results.

Nice to Haves

  • Hands-on experience with Notion as a power user or workspace admin

  • Light scripting experience or the ability to write basic examples with the Notion API

  • Experience with relational data modeling and designing information architecture

  • Exposure to RFPs, security questionnaires, and procurement processes

  • Experience in a product-led growth environment

Notion is committed to providing highly competitive cash compensation, equity, and benefits. The compensation offered for this role will be based on multiple factors such as location, the role’s scope and complexity, and the candidate’s experience and expertise, and may vary from the range provided below. For roles based in New York City, the estimated range for total on target earnings (including base salary and on target incentive pay) for this role is $195,000 - $210,000 per year.

By clicking “Submit Application”, I understand and agree that Notion and its affiliates and subsidiaries will collect and process my information in accordance with Notion’s Global Recruiting Privacy Policy and NYLL 144. #LI-Onsite

A Note on AI

You don’t need deep AI expertise for every role, but we do expect every Notino to be intellectually curious, drawn to tinkering and discovery, and excited to use AI as a real collaborator in their work. For some roles, AI fluency is a core requirement — when that’s the case, we'll say so explicitly in the qualifications. People who thrive here don’t treat AI as a novelty. They use it to think better, and make their work easier for others to build on.

Equal Opportunity & Accommodations

We hire talented people from a wide range of backgrounds. If you’re excited about this role but don’t meet every bullet, we still encourage you to apply. Notion is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of any legally protected characteristic. Consistent with applicable law, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Notion provides reasonable accommodations during the application process; if you need one, please let your recruiter know.

Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please let your recruiter know.

jobs.title: APAC Campaigns & Content Lead
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Who We Are

Notion is the collaborative AI workspace where teams and agents think together. We're building one place where your knowledge, projects, meetings, and AI tools live side by side, so work is faster, clearer, and less fragmented. Millions of individuals, small teams, and large companies run their work on Notion.

Notinos (our employees) are customer zero in bringing this future of work to life. We care about craft, building things that last, and the belief that great work is still fundamentally human. Our goal isn’t to ship the next feature. Each and every team of Notinos is working to set the standard for how humans work together in the AI era. From building a business’s system of record to making and managing AI agents to automating away the busy work, we care deeply about giving our customers more time for their life’s work.

About The Role

You’ll own APAC campaigns and content strategy and execution to drive awareness, engagement, and qualified pipeline. You will bring global campaigns and product launches into APAC by working with the local APAC marketing team to bring these campaigns to life. As well as bringing global campaigns to APAC, you will also create new campaigns from scratch and help to scale these out globally if successful in APAC. These are full integrated campaigns where you own everything from strategy, channel mix, content, messaging and execution (in-partnership with the local APAC marketing team).

This role is based in Sydney and will report to the APAC Head of Marketing, partnering closely with APAC regional and global marketing teams.

In-person collaboration is essential to Notion's culture. We require all team members to work from our offices on Mondays and Thursdays, our designated Anchor Days. Certain teams or positions may require additional in-office workdays.

What You'll Achieve

  • Lead APAC integrated campaigns from brief to launch to retro: positioning, messaging, channel plan, asset plan, and timeline management across priority markets.

  • The types of campaigns you own will be very diverse. Campaigns owned will span across product launches, demand generation campaigns, and brand campaigns.

  • Own the campaign content and assets that brings APAC campaigns to life end-to-end, including:

    • Define the content strategy, narrative, and key messages for each campaign (aligned to global positioning and regional insights).

    • Own the content plan and production of core campaign assets

    • Build and maintain modular messaging/toolkits for localization and reuse across markets (especially Japan), ensuring quality and cultural fluency.

    • Deliver Sales enablement content tied to the campaign (one-pagers, pitch decks, customer stories, talk tracks) to drive pipeline creation and conversion.

    • Create the local creative assets needed for the APAC campaigns.

  • Partner with country/regional marketers to bring a campaign to life in APAC. You will bring the APAC marketing team on your campaign journey and project plan the roll out.

  • Bring global campaigns to APAC and create new campaigns from scratch in APAC and if successful, package these up to roll out globally.

  • Own campaign performance reporting and learnings: synthesize insights (what worked/why), recommend optimizations, and share clear readouts with regional and global stakeholders.

  • Establish an AI-forward campaigns & content operating system.

Skills You'll Need to Bring

  • Proven experience leading B2B integrated campaigns and content programs in APAC, with strong judgment on “what works where” across different markets and segments.

  • Excellent messaging and storytelling skills: can turn product value into clear narratives, positioning, and multi-asset campaign copy.

  • Strong program management: can run multi-stakeholder launches with tight timelines, clear briefs, and high-quality deliverables.

  • Deep localization sensibility for APAC, especially high-context markets (e.g., Japan): you can brief, review, and raise quality to avoid “direct translation” outcomes that harm performance and brand.

  • Strong familiarity with APAC marketing/sales tech stack and measurement: Salesforce.com, Marketo/Customer.io, web analytics, UTM hygiene, and campaign reporting.

  • Extremely AI-forward: you actively adopt and operationalize AI to improve speed, quality, and consistency (research, ideation, content versioning, reporting), and can bring others along.

Nice to Haves

  • Be a Notion user

  • Also have experience across B2C campaigns

  • Experience in SaaS/Tech companies

We hire talented and passionate people from a variety of backgrounds because we want our global employee base to represent the wide diversity of our customers. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every bullet point listed in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. If you’re a builder at heart, share our company values, and enthusiastic about making software toolmaking ubiquitous, we want to hear from you.

Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. jobs.descriptionPlain:
WHO WE ARE

Notion is the collaborative AI workspace where teams and agents think together https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vkpYpWfEK5s. We're building one place where your knowledge, projects, meetings, and AI tools live side by side, so work is faster, clearer, and less fragmented. Millions of individuals, small teams, and large companies run their work on Notion.



Notinos (our employees) are customer zero in bringing this future of work to life. We care about craft, building things that last, and the belief that great work is still fundamentally human. Our goal isn’t to ship the next feature. Each and every team of Notinos is working to set the standard for how humans work together in the AI era. From building a business’s system of record to making and managing AI agents to automating away the busy work, we care deeply about giving our customers more time for their life’s work.




ABOUT THE ROLE

You’ll own APAC campaigns and content strategy and execution to drive awareness, engagement, and qualified pipeline. You will bring global campaigns and product launches into APAC by working with the local APAC marketing team to bring these campaigns to life. As well as bringing global campaigns to APAC, you will also create new campaigns from scratch and help to scale these out globally if successful in APAC. These are full integrated campaigns where you own everything from strategy, channel mix, content, messaging and execution (in-partnership with the local APAC marketing team).

This role is based in Sydney and will report to the APAC Head of Marketing, partnering closely with APAC regional and global marketing teams.

In-person collaboration is essential to Notion's culture. We require all team members to work from our offices on Mondays and Thursdays, our designated Anchor Days. Certain teams or positions may require additional in-office workdays.




WHAT YOU'LL ACHIEVE

 - Lead APAC integrated campaigns from brief to launch to retro: positioning, messaging, channel plan, asset plan, and timeline management across priority markets.

 - The types of campaigns you own will be very diverse. Campaigns owned will span across product launches, demand generation campaigns, and brand campaigns.

 - Own the campaign content and assets that brings APAC campaigns to life end-to-end, including:
   
   - Define the content strategy, narrative, and key messages for each campaign (aligned to global positioning and regional insights).
   
   - Own the content plan and production of core campaign assets
   
   - Build and maintain modular messaging/toolkits for localization and reuse across markets (especially Japan), ensuring quality and cultural fluency.
   
   - Deliver Sales enablement content tied to the campaign (one-pagers, pitch decks, customer stories, talk tracks) to drive pipeline creation and conversion.
   
   - Create the local creative assets needed for the APAC campaigns.

 - Partner with country/regional marketers to bring a campaign to life in APAC. You will bring the APAC marketing team on your campaign journey and project plan the roll out.

 - Bring global campaigns to APAC and create new campaigns from scratch in APAC and if successful, package these up to roll out globally.

 - Own campaign performance reporting and learnings: synthesize insights (what worked/why), recommend optimizations, and share clear readouts with regional and global stakeholders.

 - Establish an AI-forward campaigns & content operating system.
   
   


SKILLS YOU'LL NEED TO BRING

 - Proven experience leading B2B integrated campaigns and content programs in APAC, with strong judgment on “what works where” across different markets and segments.

 - Excellent messaging and storytelling skills: can turn product value into clear narratives, positioning, and multi-asset campaign copy.

 - Strong program management: can run multi-stakeholder launches with tight timelines, clear briefs, and high-quality deliverables.

 - Deep localization sensibility for APAC, especially high-context markets (e.g., Japan): you can brief, review, and raise quality to avoid “direct translation” outcomes that harm performance and brand.

 - Strong familiarity with APAC marketing/sales tech stack and measurement: Salesforce.com http://Salesforce.com, Marketo/Customer.io http://Customer.io, web analytics, UTM hygiene, and campaign reporting.

 - Extremely AI-forward: you actively adopt and operationalize AI to improve speed, quality, and consistency (research, ideation, content versioning, reporting), and can bring others along.
   
   


NICE TO HAVES

 - Be a Notion user

 - Also have experience across B2C campaigns

 - Experience in SaaS/Tech companies
   
   

We hire talented and passionate people from a variety of backgrounds because we want our global employee base to represent the wide diversity of our customers. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every bullet point listed in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. If you’re a builder at heart, share our company values, and enthusiastic about making software toolmaking ubiquitous, we want to hear from you.

Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please let your recruiter know.
